Why do some handwriting books make it hard for kids to write straight? 
Our upgraded 3D deep groove design gently guides the pen along the correct path, helping children trace more accurately and build proper writing habits. 
Does the disappearing ink really work? Will it leave marks after fading? 
Our magic ink fades cleanly within 20–30 minutes with no residue. The soft fiber pen tip prevents scratching, making it safe for repeated use. 
Will my child finish the workbook too quickly?

Will the pages tear easily when my child writes harder? 
We use thick, durable, no-bleed paper designed specifically for preschoolers who press harder when writing. 
What age group is this set suitable for? 
Our set is designed for ages 3–8, guiding kids from basic strokes to letters, numbers, and simple math. Perfect for preschool, kindergarten, and early elementary learners.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I recently purchased the TIME X Magic Grooved Writing Practice Books for my child, and I couldn’t be happier with the results! The reusable design is fantastic—it saves money and allows kids to practice again and again without wasting paper.What really stands out is the 3D grooved format. It gently guides little hands to follow the correct strokes, which has noticeably improved my child’s handwriting and confidence. The ink disappears after some time, making it fun and almost magical for kids to reuse the pages.The books are well-organized, covering letters, numbers, and even basic drawing, which keeps learning engaging and varied. My child actually looks forward to practice time now, which says a lot!Overall, this is an excellent learning tool for kids ages 3–8. Highly recommended for parents who want a fun, effective, and mess-free way to build early writing skills.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription:

    I have owned this short of 1 day, so I can't speak on longevity of the reusable workbooks or disappearing ink in the pens.  Nonetheless, I'll start with the bad and end with the good -Cons:-Text is very small, especially for new writers. The uppercase letters are mostly smaller than a penny. The lowercase are smaller than a dime. This is also true for the numbers.-The pages are thin enough to where you can see the grooved lines and text on the backside of the page.-It feels as though the quality is lacking to preserve costs of production, but the manufacturer is still providing enough useful tasks & books in order to justify the price point. For instance, the pages are thinner and books don't have many pages in them at all. The pens also feel like thin, cheap plastic.-The ink can be a bit unpredictable at coming out of the pens.-The pen point is sharp enough to where your child can leave writing indentations if they go outside of the grooves and press with enough pressure. It doesn't take much for this to happen (see photos).-Not many pages in each book.-Despite the workbook sizes, there are many letters and numbers to trace on each page, which my 5 year-old noticed immediately and was complaining about how many he had to trace.Pros:-The ink actually disappears!-The finger positioners are fantastic for new writers, especially my three year-old. It took her a few tries with my assistance before she got the hang of how to hold the pen properly.-There is a variety of books that are all reusable. The most important ones, like letters and numbers, are included.-The workbooks are binded together and easy to turn the pages for independent work (my 3 and 5 year-old were entertained for a bit).-The ink disappears quickly, so it's easy to go back and retrace again and again.-The grooves are just enough to allow for your little to focus to stay in the groove itself, but not be completely controlled within the groove itself. So there is effort involved there.-There are plenty of pens, so multiple kids can utilize the set together.-Everything comes in a box and is neatly stored together.-The pictures are cute and eye-catching, while also accurate.-The number workbook in particular was easy enough for my 5 year-old to do alone, while my 3 year-old was able to accurately answer the counting questions if I did them with her.-Another favorite feature is the fact the entire set of workbooks are reusable = BONUS! I can use this for multiple kids!Overall, YES, I would purchase this again! It's a great tool in my home preschool, whether the work is dependent or independent!

raw_products.bookDescription:
Workbook Features:• Ages 5-8, Kindergarten-Grade 2• 80 pages, 8 inches x 11 inches • Tracing and writing uppercase and lowercase letters, forming words and sentences, and more• Full-color practice pages with step-by-step instructions• Answer key included
Hands-On Learning: Carson Dellosa’s Handwriting: Printing Workbook helps students to learn and enhance their writing skills. Handwriting books for kids are a great way to engage students through fun tracing and writing activities.
What's Included: This hand writing practice book includes full-color practice pages covering letter sounds, letter recognition, and beginner’s handwriting skills through tracing and writing activities. An answer key is also included.
How It Works: Students write, trace, and sound out letters, and form words and sentences through interactive writing activities. The full-color pages feature clear, step-by-step instructions to follow along with each new writing task.
Working Together: Parents love the workbook for added practice from home and teachers love incorporating it into hands-on writing activities. Help your child understand each activity by reading directions and check their progress with the answer key.
Why Carson Dellosa: For more than 45 years, Carson Dellosa has provided solutions for parents and teachers to help their children get ahead and exceed learning goals. Carson Dellosa supports your child’s educational journey every step of the way.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Unique and to the point workbook to practice handwriting :)
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Our daughter is 3 and half and she needed the right way to practice letters. First we taught her writing letters started on Doodle board but after a certain time we needed something unique and best to teach her how to write within lines. In our times we used to practice letters in notebooks with margins  and blue and red lines. We searched the same one but in a workbook format and voila we got this Handwriting: Printing Workbook (Brighter Child: Grades K-2 in the same old but best  style.The super points about this book -1) Fast delivery - It came so fast.2) Good Build quality- The page quality is really good and does not tear easily. No marks or smudges left after erasing letters. So I can reuse the same page.3)Features- The workbook  has 3 stages for practicing handwriting .a) The first stage is for practicing writing letters from A-Z. Each letter is given in a single page in both upper and lower cases.Each letter is shown how to write in traced format in first row. Then you are given a few more lines to practice those. So Practice by tracing the letters and then write the letters. Practice makes one perfect . This is the bottom line of this workbook. During practice each child easily knows the concept of how to write uppercase and lowercase within top and bottom blue and middle red dashed lines.b) The second stage is for practicing writing words. Here also the same motto. After looking at an example and tracing it, you practice it independently. First practice by tracing the words the write the words.This workbook nicely graduates a child's writing from letters to words.c) The third stage is for practicing sentences. The sentences became long gradually.This book is not only for practicing handwriting it also boosts up a child, self enhances and make him/her independent learner during the vast kingdom of letters and word and sentences.Each page of word and sentence practice has beautiful pictures drawn associated with the word to be written. This will surely be lovable by a young writer.My daughter already knew how to write each letters in both upper and lower cases. But she needed the right direction to write them in an organized way and she got it through this.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: The Best Handwriting Handbook On the Market for Kindergarten
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I can't tell you how excited and blown away I am by this handwriting workbook, "BIG Handwriting Practice Workbook For Kids," By Crystal Radke. I am always on the look out for high-quality and reputable learning materials to supplement learning at home.  Although I am unfamiliar with the publisher and the author, I went ahead and purchased this for my newly minted kindergartner. When this book came in the mail, I thought it was like any other worksheet book that I had purchased in the past. But when I really started to review what was inside, I was actually very impressed at how educationally dense the material was and how well the learning material was presented.My child has been in school for the better of seven weeks and I am personally alarmed at how quickly my child's classroom is flying through foundational handwriting material. More, now than ever, I feel as though there is a lot of pressure on parents to get their kinder aged children ready and able to read and write at this age when it is developmentally challenging (and inappropriate) for most children at this age. My child is extremely bright and confident but has gross and fine hand strength delay. I am always looking for supplemental work to re- enforce what they learn in school and in occupational therapy, but to make it fun, joyful and low stress on them. The material covered in this workbook is STAND-OUT and very age appropriate. What I think most parents don't understand is that there is significant pre-writing skills that need to be practiced and this is included in the first portion of this workbook. The worksheets are colorful and bright with modern printing and pictures all throughout the engaging material. I love that as your child masters the alphabet handwriting, each letter has a comprehensive review to show that there is understanding. This is very reminiscent of the homework and worksheets that my child receives from their teacher and brings home. I like that the letters are separately presented in uppercase and lower case form. I think that is quite clever as you can focus on the the difference of each form.  There is also number tracing and coordinating math problems as well! One of my favorite inclusions of this book is the CVC and sight word chapter and the opportunity for kids to see, trace, write and become familiar with them. Sight words are becoming such a huge component of kindergarten level reading and writing, and I cannot stress enough how important it is for your child to be actively learning sight words. I love that in the back of the book, there is a tracker that your child can color in to show mastery of the specific letters and numbers they have completed. At the completion of the the book, you can fill out the the certificate of completion and I think that is so special for giving young learners accolades for all of their hardwork.As the education system (private or public) continues to push reading and writing on kindergarten learners, I felt as a parent that this workbook material was more attune to the current trends that parents and students are being presented with. I hope that this author comes out with more educational material in the future because I will purchase without hesitation.

raw_products.bookDescription:
Penmanship lessons that feel like play for kids ages 6-9
Printing neatly takes practice, but it can be hard to keep children engaged with ordinary writing drills. This innovative penmanship workbook teaches children good penmanship as they read and copy words that are all about animals. With colorful illustrations and fun facts about mammals, fish, reptiles, birds, and bugs, each writing lesson feels like a romp through the animal kingdom!
Kids will build skills as they go, beginning with tracing the alphabet and working up to printing full sentences. Each exercise uncovers new knowledge, like "an elephant's tusks never stop growing" and "there are more kangaroos than humans in Australia." Full-page pictures to color offer an opportunity to get creative in between writing lessons. A Before and After page highlights progress, showing kids how much their penmanship has improved.
Go beyond traditional, repetitive exercises with a penmanship workbook that makes handwriting wildly entertaining!

aPlusContent.title: From the Publisher
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the Publisher
Practicing penmanship is fun with animal facts for kids ages 6-9 
Learning good penmanship doesn’t have to be boring! This workbook is packed with full-color pictures and tons of animal facts to engage kids’ brains as they practice writing letters, words, and sentences. 
Build skills progressively 
Practice one skill after another by tracing letters and words first before writing them independently. 
Learn about animals 
Explore a wild world of facts about animals, fish, reptiles, birds, and bugs that make every lesson fun. 
Celebrate progress 
A “before and after” writing sample shows off how much kids’ penmanship has improved!

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This was a really fun and engaging book. It's also good quality paper and binding, which I didn't necessarily expect for a workbook. I honestly may repurchase it...
